```python
class Solution:
    def minMovesToMakePalindrome(self, s: str) -> int:
        s = list(s)
        res = 0
        n = len(s)
        left, right = 0, n - 1
        while left < right:
            if s[left] == s[right]:
                left += 1
                right -= 1
            else:
                p = right
                while s[left] != s[p] and p > left:
                    p -= 1
                if p == left:
                    s[p], s[p + 1] = s[p + 1], s[p]
                    res += 1
                else:
                    while p < right:
                        s[p], s[p + 1] = s[p + 1], s[p]
                        p += 1
                        res += 1
                    left += 1
                    right -= 1
        return res
```

efficiency: HIGH  
time complexity: O(n^2)  
space complexity: O(n)  
energy implications: LOW  
explanation: The solution uses a two-pointer technique, starting from both ends of the string and moving towards the center. It checks for matching characters and swaps adjacent characters when necessary, counting the number of moves. The main strength lies in its simplicity and effectiveness in handling the given constraints.
